55: 
56: ## Complete Repository Analysis
57: 
58: ### 1. Project Type and Framework
59: 
60: **Project:** "Bolao" / "Sweepstake" -- a CLI-based football (soccer) prediction sweepstake analysis engine.
61: 
62: **Not a web app.** There is no web framework (no Flask, Django, FastAPI). It is a **Python CLI application** that:
63: - Reads Excel files containing participants' match predictions
64: - Fetches real match results from external CSV sources
65: - Runs a **medallion pipeline** (bronze -> silver -> gold) to clean, merge, and score predictions
66: - Generates **static HTML reports** (mobile-first, self-contained) for offline viewing
67: - Uses `matplotlib`, `pandas`, `streamlit` (listed but not used in current code), `openpyxl`, `xlrd`, `tabulate`, `pytz`, `pyyaml`, `requests`
68: 
69: **CLI entry point:** `ss` command (defined in `cli.py`, registered in `pyproject.toml` as `ss = "cli:main"`).
70: 
71: **CLI commands:**
72: - `ss list` -- List available championships
73: - `ss run <championship>` -- Run full pipeline + HTML reports
74: - `ss run <championship> --pipeline` -- Run only data pipeline
75: - `ss run <championship> --reports` -- Run only report generation
76: - `ss run <championship> --bronze/--silver/--gold` -- Run single pipeline stage
77: - `ss fetch-logos <championship>` -- Download team logo images
78: 
79: **Championships configured:**
80: - `2025_club_world_cup` (48 group matches, 32 teams, oitavas-quartas-semi-final)
81: - `2026_world_cup` (72 group matches, 48 teams, segunda_fase-oitavas-quartas-semi-terceiro_lugar-final)
82: 
83: ---
84: 
85: ### 2. Data Models / Entities
86: 
87: Defined in `src/core/config.py`:
88: 
89: | Data Class | Fields | Purpose |
90: |---|---|---|
91: | `ScoringRule` | `name`, `points`, `priority`, `rule`, `emoji`, `description`, `color_text`, `max_total_error`, `min_total_error` | A single scoring criterion |
92: | `PlayoffRound` | `name`, `key`, `matches` | A knockout round definition |
93: | `ThemeColors` | ~40 color fields (hex/rgba) | Color palette for HTML reports |
94: | `ThemeConfig` | `mode`, `colors: ThemeColors` | Full theme configuration |
95: | `FirstRoundLayout` | `matches`, `skiprows` | Group stage Excel slicing config |
96: | `PlayoffsLayout` | `striker_row_offset`, `striker_label`, `striker_name_column`, `striker_name_fallback_column`, `champion_cell`, `name_split_char`, `name_split_index`, `rounds: list[dict]`, `playoffs_sheet_name` | Playoff Excel slicing config |
97: | `ExcelLayout` | `first_round: FirstRoundLayout`, `playoffs: PlayoffsLayout` | How to slice the Excel sheet |
98: | `ChampionshipConfig` | `id`, `name`, `year`, `timezone`, `scoring_rules`, `playoff_rounds`, `excel_layout`, `base_dir`, `raw_dir`, `results_file`, `games_file`, `bronze_dir`, `silver_dir`, `gold_dir`, `reports_dir`, `results_endpoint`, `team_name_mapping`, `team_logos`, `team_slugs`, `report_title`, `group_phase_label`, `theme`, `playoff_scoring`, `actual_top_scorer`, `striker_points`, `groups`, `standings_format`, `standings_skiprows`, `bonus_team_picks`, `striker_pick` | Full config for a single championship |
99: 
100: **Data files (per championship):**
101: - `games.csv` -- Official match results
102: - Raw Excel files in `data/raw/` -- Participant prediction sheets
103: - Bronze layer: `data/bronze/first_round/group_phase_{boleiro}.csv`, `bonus_teams_{boleiro}.csv`, `striker_{boleiro}.csv`
104: - Silver layer: `data/silver/first_round/group_phase_{boleiro}.csv` (predictions merged with results)
105: - Gold layer: `data/gold/first_round/group_phase_{boleiro}.csv`, `{label}_all.csv`, `{label}_valido_all.csv`, `striker_{boleiro}.csv`, `playoffs_strikers.csv`, plus 10+ analytics CSVs
106: 
107: **Gold analytics CSVs generated by pipeline:**
108: - `playoffs_scored.csv` -- Bonus team pick scoring
109: - `strikers_scored.csv` -- Striker pick scoring
110: - `consistency.csv` -- Streak/consistency tracking
111: - `upset_tracker.csv` -- Upset match identification
112: - `round_by_round.csv` -- Points per round per player
113: - `team_accuracy.csv` -- Prediction accuracy per team
114: - `ranking_history.csv` -- Daily rank position
115: - `boldness_index.csv` -- Boldness index per player
116: - `prediction_timing.csv` -- When each player submitted picks
117: - `goal_error_by_team.csv` -- MAE per team per player
118: - `group_standings.csv` -- Real tournament group standings
119: 
120: ---
121: 
122: ### 3. Complete List of ALL Generated HTML Pages/Views
123: 
124: The `generate_html_reports()` function in `src/core/reports/html.py` (line 4079) and `generate_dashboard()` in `src/core/reports/dashboard.py` (line 1160) generate the following pages:
125: 
126: #### Main Pages (generated in `src/reports/{championship}/html/`)
127: 
128: | Page | Filename | Builder Function | What It Displays |
129: |---|---|---|---|
130: | **Dashboard / Home** | `index.html` | `generate_dashboard()` in `dashboard.py` | Hero banner with title, zebra counter, emoji legend, live games, last result card, 3x3 grid of quick links, scoring emoji accordion, full ranking table with badges (hot streak, zebra hunter, leader, boldness), bonus times leaderboard, upcoming games, phase-by-phase game buttons, bottom navigation bar |
131: | **Player Profile** | `boleiros/{name}.html` | `_build_boleiro()` in `html.py` | Per-player stats (total points, avg, aproveitamento), scoring distribution bar chart, bonus teams card with phase-by-phase advancing team picks, striker/champion badges, scoring criteria distribution table, day-by-day timeline bars, player-vs-bolao comparison bars, pending match notices, full match history with zebra indicators |
132: | **Match Page** | `jogos/{phase}/{date}_{hour}_{slug}.html` | `_build_match()` in `html.py` | Per-match prediction analysis: score card with team logos, CSS heatmap of all player predictions (home goals x away goals grid), per-player prediction rows with points, criteria, zebra badges, match timing info |
133: | **Arena (Player Comparison)** | `arena.html` | `_build_arena()` in `html.py` | Dropdown select two players, side-by-side stat comparison (total, avg, games, bonus), daily bar chart comparison, cumulative line chart comparison, recent results list |
134: | **Zebras** | `zebras.html` | `_build_zebras()` in `html.py` | Upset match cards (zebra magnitude: Zebra Monstra / Zebra Grande), explanation of zebra rule, summary stats (total matches, # of zebras, %), "Rei das Zebras" leaderboard, difficulty ranking of matches with bars, "Favorites that Won" section |
135: | **Palpites (Predictions Grid)** | `palpites.html` | `_build_round_predictions()` in `new_views.py` | Full predictions grid with all games as columns, all players as rows. Each cell shows predicted score + points earned. Multi-game selector via dropdown menus with checkboxes per round. JavaScript-driven filtering, recalculates totals. Default: last 10 completed + next upcoming. |
136: | **Raio-X (X-Ray)** | `bolao_xray.html` | `_build_bolao_xray()` in `html.py` | Meta-analysis of the entire bolao: snapshot stats (#players, #matches, #predictions, total pts, avg pts, overall aproveitamento), full ranking table with medal icons, heatmap matrix (players x days, color = % of max possible points), "Match of the Day" cards (most correct, most wrong, most divided), most/least predictable matches table |
137: | **Rodadas (Round Matrix)** | `rodadas.html` | `_build_round_matrix()` in `new_views.py` | Round-by-round comparison matrix: rows=players, cols=rounds, cells show points + rank. Players sorted by total. Top scorers per round (podium style). |
138: | **Times (Team Guide)** | `times.html` | `_build_times_index()` in `new_views.py` | Index page listing all teams with their group and a link to each team's detail page |
139: | **Team Page** | `times/{team}.html` | `_build_team_page()` in `new_views.py` | Per-team analytics: group info, record (W-D-L, GF-GA), player accuracy table (correct winner, exact score, %), MAE error bars per player with bias indicator, match history with zebra indicators |
140: | **Tabela Real (Real Standings)** | `tabela_real.html` | `_build_group_standings()` in `new_views.py` | Real tournament group standings table (PJ, V, E, D, GP, GC, SG, Pts), advancing teams highlighted, knockout bracket visualization |
141: | **Similaridade (Similarity Matrix)** | `similaridade.html` | `_build_similarity_matrix()` in `new_views.py` | Player-vs-player similarity: heat map matrix showing % winner agreement between each pair, ranked table of most similar pairs (winner %, score %) |
142: | **Ranking Evolution** | `ranking_evolution.html` | `_build_ranking_evolution()` in `html.py` | Interactive Canvas line chart: toggleable per-player display of rank position over time (inverted Y-axis), tooltip on hover, current standings table with leader distance |
143: | **Boldometro** | `boldometer.html` | `_build_boldometer()` in `html.py` | Interactive Canvas scatter plot: x-axis = boldness (avg goals vs bolao avg), y-axis = avg points per game. 4 quadrants (Efficient/Risky/Conservative/Bold). Tooltip on hover with player profile. Explanation section. Top-3 boldest players table. |
144: | **Momentum** | `momentum.html` | `_build_momentum()` in `html.py` | Current streaks per player (hot/cold) with visual bars, longest streak records table, podium for hottest and coldest current streaks, explanation of streak rules |
145: | **Day Winners** | `day_winners.html` | `_build_day_winners()` in `html.py` | Per-day match analysis: the match with most correct predictions, most wrong predictions, and most divided predictions for each game day |
146: 
147: #### Navigation
148: 
149: All pages share a **bottom navigation bar** with 5 links:
150: - `index.html` (Inicio)
151: - `arena.html` (Arena)
152: - `zebras.html` (Zebras)
153: - `palpites.html` (Palpites)
154: - `bolao_xray.html` (Raio-X)
155: 
156: The dashboard `index.html` also has a **3x3 quick-link grid**:
157: - `tabela_real.html` (Grupos)
158: - `rodadas.html` (Rodadas)
159: - `times.html` (Times)
160: - `similaridade.html` (Sosia)
161: - `palpites.html` (Palpites)
162: - `ranking_evolution.html` (Evolucao)
163: - `boldometer.html` (Boldometro)
164: - `zebras.html` (Zebras)
165: - `bolao_xray.html` (Raio-X)
166: 
167: ---
168: 
169: ### 4. Chart / Visualization Libraries Used
170: 
171: **No external charting libraries (no Chart.js, D3.js, Plotly, etc.).** All visualizations are implemented using:
172: 
173: 1. **HTML/CSS bar charts** -- Pure CSS `<div>` elements with percentage widths for bar charts (distribution, comparisons, difficulty rankings)
174: 2. **HTML/CSS heatmaps** -- Grid of colored `<div>` elements for the player x day heatmap and the per-match prediction heatmap
175: 3. **HTML/CSS tables** -- For all ranking tables, matrices, and data grids (with zebra striping, rank highlights via CSS classes)
176: 4. **HTML `<canvas>`** -- Using **raw JavaScript Canvas API** for two interactive charts:
177:    - `ranking_evolution.html` -- Line chart of rank position over time with toggleable players, tooltips, grid lines
178:    - `boldometer.html` -- Scatter plot with quadrant backgrounds, dot rendering with initials, tooltip on hover
179: 
180: The JavaScript code is inline within each HTML page (no external JS files). The styling uses CSS custom properties driven by the championship's `ThemeConfig` (dark theme by default, mobile-first responsive design).
181: 
182: There is also **client-side JavaScript** for:
183: - Dropdown filter menus with checkboxes in `palpites.html`
184: - Player comparison selector in `arena.html`
185: - Toggleable player lines in `ranking_evolution.html`
186: - Interactive hover tooltips in charts
187: 
188: **Backend Python visualization:** `matplotlib>=3.10.3` is listed as a dependency in `pyproject.toml` but is **not currently used** in any of the report generation code. It may be used for future or optional features. `streamlit>=1.45.1` is also listed but **not used** in the current codebase.

237: Possible new views that don't repeat existing ones:
238: 
239: 1. **"Cenarios" (Scenarios)** - What-if analysis: "If remaining matches go a certain way, who would win?" - This is a classic in bolao/sweepstakes. Show possible final rankings based on different scenarios.

353: The user chose "Arquétipos (Player Personas)" - classifying each player into archetypes based on their prediction patterns.

355: Let me think about this more deeply. I need to plan what data to use and how to build this.
356: 
357: Available data per player:
358: - Boldness index (boldness_index.csv) - how much their score predictions deviate from the average
359: - Consistency (consistency.csv) - streaks, consistency of performance
360: - Zebras hit (upset_tracker.csv) - did they predict upsets?
361: - Team accuracy (team_accuracy.csv) - which teams they predict well
362: - Goal error by team (goal_error_by_team.csv) - MAE per team
363: - Round-by-round points (round_by_round.csv) - performance over time
364: - Scoring distribution per player
365: - Prediction timing (prediction_timing.csv) - when they submit
366: - Overall stats: points, avg, aproveitamento
367: 
368: Possible archetypes:
369: 
370: 1. **O Favoriteiro (The Favorite Follower)** - Always picks favorites, rarely hits zebras, low boldness, high accuracy on favorites
371: 2. **O Zebreiro (The Zebra Hunter)** - Loves predicting upsets, high boldness, low overall accuracy but high zebra count
372: 3. **O Otimista (The Optimist)** - Always predicts lots of goals (high scoring predictions), high goal error overestimation
373: 4. **O Pessimista (The Pessimist)** - Predicts low-scoring games, underestimates goals
374: 5. **O Conservador (The Conservative)** - Predicts close to the average, low boldness, medium accuracy
375: 6. **O Cientista (The Scientist)** - Most accurate overall, data-driven predictions, consistent
376: 7. **O Tardio (The Latecomer)** - Submits predictions last minute, varies in accuracy
377: 8. **O Madrugador (The Early Bird)** - Submits predictions well in advance
378: 9. **O Especialista (The Specialist)** - Very good with specific teams, bad with others
379: 10. **O Consistente (The Consistent)** - Always scores around the same, no wild swings
380:

666: 
667: ### Key columns for classifying player archetypes
668: 
669: For **player archetype classification**, the most useful gold-layer files and their columns are:
670: 
671: | File | Key archetype signals |
672: |---|---|
673: | **`boldness_index.csv`** | `boldness_score`, `avg_total_goals_bol`, `extreme_score_pct` -- risk-seeking vs conservative predictor |
674: | **`consistency.csv`** | `streak_type`, `streak_length`, `running_avg_5` -- streaky vs steady performer |
675: | **`upset_tracker.csv`** | `is_upset`, `num_correct` -- contrarian vs consensus-follower |
676: | **`team_accuracy.csv`** | `accuracy_pct`, `correct_winner`, `exact_score` -- precision profile per team/role |
677: | **`goal_error_by_team.csv`** | `mae`, `goal_bias` -- over-predictor vs under-predictor per team |
678: | **`round_by_round.csv`** | `points`, `cumulative_points`, `rank` -- early starter vs late bloomer |
679: | **`prediction_timing.csv`** | `lead_days` -- early submitter vs last-minute predictor |
680: | **`playoffs_strikers.csv`** | `striker` -- favorite player choice (star-chaser vs unique pick) |
681: | **`1afase_all.csv` / `1afase_valido_all.csv`** | `criterio`, `pontos`, and the `1-...` through `9-...` one-hot flags -- exact-score specialist vs safe-bet player |
682: </task_result>
683: </task>

687: 
688: ### 🤖 AGENTE (Turno 4):
689: 
690: 💭 **PENSAMENTO DO AGENTE:**
691: Now I have a very comprehensive understanding of the data available. Let me design the "Arquétipos (Player Personas)" view.
692: 
693: The idea is to classify each player into a persona based on their prediction patterns. I'll use multiple data signals.
694: 
695: Here are the archetypes I can define based on available data:
696: 
697: ### Proposed Archetypes
698: 
699: 1. **O Zebreiro (The Zebra Hunter)** 🦓
700:    - Signs: High number of zebras predicted correctly, high boldness score, low crowd-agreement
701:    - Data: upset_tracker (players_correct count), boldness_index (boldness_score)
702: 
703: 2. **O Cientista (The Scientist)** 🧪
704:    - Signs: Highest overall accuracy, consistent performance, balanced predictions
705:    - Data: 1afase_valido_all (avg pontos), consistency (running_avg), team_accuracy
706: 
707: 3. **O Conservador (The Conservative)** 🛡️
708:    - Signs: Low boldness score, near-average predictions, few extremes
709:    - Data: boldness_index (low boldness_score, low extreme_score_pct)
710: 
711: 4. **O Otimista (The Optimist)** 🌟
712:    - Signs: Predicts more goals than average, positive goal_bias
713:    - Data: boldness_index (high avg_total_goals_bol), goal_error_by_team (positive goal_bias on average)
714: 
715: 5. **O Pessimista (The Pessimist)** ☁️
716:    - Signs: Predicts fewer goals than average, negative goal_bias
717:    - Data: boldness_index (low avg_total_goals_bol), goal_error_by_team (negative goal_bias on average)
718: 
719: 6. **O Especialista (The Specialist)** 🎯
720:    - Signs: Very high accuracy on specific teams, low on others, high variance in team accuracy
721:    - Data: team_accuracy (high variance in accuracy_pct across teams)
722: 
723: 7. **O Consistente (The Consistent One)** 📊
724:    - Signs: Low variance in points per round, steady rank, long hit streaks
725:    - Data: round_by_round (low variance in points), consistency (longest hit streak)
726: 
727: 8. **O Montanha-Russa (The Rollercoaster)** 🎢
728:    - Signs: High variance in points per round, alternates hot/cold streaks
729:    - Data: round_by_round (high variance in points), consistency (alternating streak types)
730: 
731: 9. **O Madrugador (The Early Bird)** 🐦
732:    - Signs: Submits predictions many days before first match
733:    - Data: prediction_timing (high lead_days)
734: 
735: 10. **O Tardio (The Latecomer)** ⏰
736:     - Signs: Submits predictions last minute / after first match started
737:     - Data: prediction_timing (low or zero lead_days)
738: 
739: 11. **O Placarzeiro (The Exact-Score Specialist)** 🎯
740:     - Signs: Higher than average proportion of exact-score hits
741:     - Data: 1afase_valido_all (count of "1-Placar exato" = True), or proportion of exact scores
742: 
743: 12. **O Craque (The Star)** 👑
744:     - Signs: Overall top performer - high rank, high points, high accuracy across the board
745:     - Data: multiple signals
746: 
747: Actually, some of these are too many. Let me refine to a core set of ~7-8 that are most distinct and data-supported:
748: 
749: 1. **O Cientista** - Highest accuracy, balanced, consistent top performer
750: 2. **O Zebreiro** - Zebra hunter, contrarian, high boldness
751: 3. **O Conservador** - Safe predictions, low boldness, follows the crowd
752: 4. **O Otimista** - Predicts goal-fests, overestimates goals
753: 5. **O Pessimista** - Predicts low scores, underestimates
754: 6. **O Especialista** - Niche expert in specific teams
755: 7. **O Montanha-Russa** - Wild swings in performance
756: 8. **O Placarzeiro** - Exact-score specialist
757: 
758: And each player gets a PRIMARY archetype (the one that fits best) plus secondary traits shown as badges.
759: 
760: Let me now plan the implementation:
761: 
762: ### Plan
763: 
764: 1. New view: **Arquétipos.html** (arquetipos.html)
765: 2. Python generator in `src/core/reports/new_views.py` (or a new file)
766: 3. Uses gold data to classify each player
767: 4. Display:
768:    - Header: "Arquétipos do Bolão" with explanation
769:    - Grid of player cards, each showing:
770:      - Player name and avatar/initial
771:      - Primary archetype with emoji and name
772:      - Archetype description (1-2 lines)
773:      - Archetype score (how strongly they fit, 0-100%)
774:      - Key stats that support the classification
775:      - Secondary traits as small badges
776:    - Maybe a radar/spider chart per player showing their trait scores
777: 5. Bottom nav + link from dashboard
778: 
779: Let me now think about the math for classification:
780: 
781: #### Classification Logic
782: 
783: **Boldness Score** (from boldness_index.csv):
784: - Normalize boldness_score across players (0-1)
785: - High = Zebreiro/Otimista tendency
786: - Low = Conservador/Pessimista tendency
787: 
788: **Goal Bias** (from goal_error_by_team.csv):
789: - Average goal_bias across all teams per player
790: - Positive = Otimista
791: - Negative = Pessimista
792: 
793: **Consistency** (from consistency.csv):
794: - Variance of running_avg_5 - high variance = Montanha-Russa
795: - Longest hit streak = Consistente
796: - Ratio of hit to miss streaks
797: 
798: **Team Accuracy Variance** (from team_accuracy.csv):
799: - Variance of accuracy_pct across teams
800: - High = Especialista (good with some, bad with others)
801: - Low = Generalista
802: 
803: **Exact Score Rate** (from 1afase_valido_all.csv):
804: - Count of "1-Placar exato" / total matches
805: - High = Placarzeiro
806: 
807: **Zebra Rate** (from upset_tracker.csv):
808: - Count of upsets they predicted correctly
809: - High = Zebreiro
810: 
811: Let me plan the file structure:
812: 
813: - `src/core/reports/arquetipos.py` - New module with classification logic + HTML generation
814: - Or add to `src/core/reports/new_views.py`
815: 
816: Actually, looking at the existing code structure, the HTML generation is split:
817: - `src/core/reports/html.py` - Main HTML generation
818: - `src/core/reports/new_views.py` - Newer views (times, palpites grid, rodadas, similarity, group standings)
819: - `src/core/reports/dashboard.py` - Dashboard
820: 
821: I think adding to `new_views.py` makes sense since it's the newer views file, OR a new file `arquetipos.py`.
822: 
823: Now let me present the plan to the user.
824: 
825: Let me think about something more concrete. The user wants something "novo mas que nao se repita". The archetype view is novel because:
826: 1. No existing view classifies players into behavioral personas
827: 2. It synthesizes data from many different CSVs into a single classification
828: 3. It tells a story about each player's style, not just their score
829: 4. It's fun and engaging - people love being categorized

54: ---
55: 
56: ## Complete Repository Analysis
57: 
58: ### 1. Project Type and Framework
59: 
60: **Project:** "Bolao" / "Sweepstake" -- a CLI-based football (soccer) prediction sweepstake analysis engine.
61: 
62: **Not a web app.** There is no web framework (no Flask, Django, FastAPI). It is a **Python CLI application** that:
63: - Reads Excel files containing participants' match predictions
64: - Fetches real match results from external CSV sources
65: - Runs a **medallion pipeline** (bronze -> silver -> gold) to clean, merge, and score predictions
66: - Generates **static HTML reports** (mobile-first, self-contained) for offline viewing
67: - Uses `matplotlib`, `pandas`, `streamlit` (listed but not used in current code), `openpyxl`, `xlrd`, `tabulate`, `pytz`, `pyyaml`, `requests`
68: 
69: **CLI entry point:** `ss` command (defined in `cli.py`, registered in `pyproject.toml` as `ss = "cli:main"`).
70: 
71: **CLI commands:**
72: - `ss list` -- List available championships
73: - `ss run <championship>` -- Run full pipeline + HTML reports
74: - `ss run <championship> --pipeline` -- Run only data pipeline
75: - `ss run <championship> --reports` -- Run only report generation
76: - `ss run <championship> --bronze/--silver/--gold` -- Run single pipeline stage
77: - `ss fetch-logos <championship>` -- Download team logo images
78: 
79: **Championships configured:**
80: - `2025_club_world_cup` (48 group matches, 32 teams, oitavas-quartas-semi-final)
81: - `2026_world_cup` (72 group matches, 48 teams, segunda_fase-oitavas-quartas-semi-terceiro_lugar-final)
82: 
83: ---
84: 
85: ### 2. Data Models / Entities
86: 
87: Defined in `src/core/config.py`:
88: 
89: | Data Class | Fields | Purpose |
90: |---|---|---|
91: | `ScoringRule` | `name`, `points`, `priority`, `rule`, `emoji`, `description`, `color_text`, `max_total_error`, `min_total_error` | A single scoring criterion |
92: | `PlayoffRound` | `name`, `key`, `matches` | A knockout round definition |
93: | `ThemeColors` | ~40 color fields (hex/rgba) | Color palette for HTML reports |
94: | `ThemeConfig` | `mode`, `colors: ThemeColors` | Full theme configuration |
95: | `FirstRoundLayout` | `matches`, `skiprows` | Group stage Excel slicing config |
96: | `PlayoffsLayout` | `striker_row_offset`, `striker_label`, `striker_name_column`, `striker_name_fallback_column`, `champion_cell`, `name_split_char`, `name_split_index`, `rounds: list[dict]`, `playoffs_sheet_name` | Playoff Excel slicing config |
97: | `ExcelLayout` | `first_round: FirstRoundLayout`, `playoffs: PlayoffsLayout` | How to slice the Excel sheet |
98: | `ChampionshipConfig` | `id`, `name`, `year`, `timezone`, `scoring_rules`, `playoff_rounds`, `excel_layout`, `base_dir`, `raw_dir`, `results_file`, `games_file`, `bronze_dir`, `silver_dir`, `gold_dir`, `reports_dir`, `results_endpoint`, `team_name_mapping`, `team_logos`, `team_slugs`, `report_title`, `group_phase_label`, `theme`, `playoff_scoring`, `actual_top_scorer`, `striker_points`, `groups`, `standings_format`, `standings_skiprows`, `bonus_team_picks`, `striker_pick` | Full config for a single championship |
99: 
100: **Data files (per championship):**
101: - `games.csv` -- Official match results
102: - Raw Excel files in `data/raw/` -- Participant prediction sheets
103: - Bronze layer: `data/bronze/first_round/group_phase_{boleiro}.csv`, `bonus_teams_{boleiro}.csv`, `striker_{boleiro}.csv`
104: - Silver layer: `data/silver/first_round/group_phase_{boleiro}.csv` (predictions merged with results)
105: - Gold layer: `data/gold/first_round/group_phase_{boleiro}.csv`, `{label}_all.csv`, `{label}_valido_all.csv`, `striker_{boleiro}.csv`, `playoffs_strikers.csv`, plus 10+ analytics CSVs
106: 
107: **Gold analytics CSVs generated by pipeline:**
108: - `playoffs_scored.csv` -- Bonus team pick scoring
109: - `strikers_scored.csv` -- Striker pick scoring
110: - `consistency.csv` -- Streak/consistency tracking
111: - `upset_tracker.csv` -- Upset match identification
112: - `round_by_round.csv` -- Points per round per player
113: - `team_accuracy.csv` -- Prediction accuracy per team
114: - `ranking_history.csv` -- Daily rank position
115: - `boldness_index.csv` -- Boldness index per player
116: - `prediction_timing.csv` -- When each player submitted picks
117: - `goal_error_by_team.csv` -- MAE per team per player
118: - `group_standings.csv` -- Real tournament group standings
119: 
120: ---
121: 
122: ### 3. Complete List of ALL Generated HTML Pages/Views
123: 
124: The `generate_html_reports()` function in `src/core/reports/html.py` (line 4079) and `generate_dashboard()` in `src/core/reports/dashboard.py` (line 1160) generate the following pages:
125: 
126: #### Main Pages (generated in `src/reports/{championship}/html/`)
127: 
128: | Page | Filename | Builder Function | What It Displays |
129: |---|---|---|---|
130: | **Dashboard / Home** | `index.html` | `generate_dashboard()` in `dashboard.py` | Hero banner with title, zebra counter, emoji legend, live games, last result card, 3x3 grid of quick links, scoring emoji accordion, full ranking table with badges (hot streak, zebra hunter, leader, boldness), bonus times leaderboard, upcoming games, phase-by-phase game buttons, bottom navigation bar |
131: | **Player Profile** | `boleiros/{name}.html` | `_build_boleiro()` in `html.py` | Per-player stats (total points, avg, aproveitamento), scoring distribution bar chart, bonus teams card with phase-by-phase advancing team picks, striker/champion badges, scoring criteria distribution table, day-by-day timeline bars, player-vs-bolao comparison bars, pending match notices, full match history with zebra indicators |
132: | **Match Page** | `jogos/{phase}/{date}_{hour}_{slug}.html` | `_build_match()` in `html.py` | Per-match prediction analysis: score card with team logos, CSS heatmap of all player predictions (home goals x away goals grid), per-player prediction rows with points, criteria, zebra badges, match timing info |
133: | **Arena (Player Comparison)** | `arena.html` | `_build_arena()` in `html.py` | Dropdown select two players, side-by-side stat comparison (total, avg, games, bonus), daily bar chart comparison, cumulative line chart comparison, recent results list |
134: | **Zebras** | `zebras.html` | `_build_zebras()` in `html.py` | Upset match cards (zebra magnitude: Zebra Monstra / Zebra Grande), explanation of zebra rule, summary stats (total matches, # of zebras, %), "Rei das Zebras" leaderboard, difficulty ranking of matches with bars, "Favorites that Won" section |
135: | **Palpites (Predictions Grid)** | `palpites.html` | `_build_round_predictions()` in `new_views.py` | Full predictions grid with all games as columns, all players as rows. Each cell shows predicted score + points earned. Multi-game selector via dropdown menus with checkboxes per round. JavaScript-driven filtering, recalculates totals. Default: last 10 completed + next upcoming. |
136: | **Raio-X (X-Ray)** | `bolao_xray.html` | `_build_bolao_xray()` in `html.py` | Meta-analysis of the entire bolao: snapshot stats (#players, #matches, #predictions, total pts, avg pts, overall aproveitamento), full ranking table with medal icons, heatmap matrix (players x days, color = % of max possible points), "Match of the Day" cards (most correct, most wrong, most divided), most/least predictable matches table |
137: | **Rodadas (Round Matrix)** | `rodadas.html` | `_build_round_matrix()` in `new_views.py` | Round-by-round comparison matrix: rows=players, cols=rounds, cells show points + rank. Players sorted by total. Top scorers per round (podium style). |
138: | **Times (Team Guide)** | `times.html` | `_build_times_index()` in `new_views.py` | Index page listing all teams with their group and a link to each team's detail page |
139: | **Team Page** | `times/{team}.html` | `_build_team_page()` in `new_views.py` | Per-team analytics: group info, record (W-D-L, GF-GA), player accuracy table (correct winner, exact score, %), MAE error bars per player with bias indicator, match history with zebra indicators |
140: | **Tabela Real (Real Standings)** | `tabela_real.html` | `_build_group_standings()` in `new_views.py` | Real tournament group standings table (PJ, V, E, D, GP, GC, SG, Pts), advancing teams highlighted, knockout bracket visualization |
141: | **Similaridade (Similarity Matrix)** | `similaridade.html` | `_build_similarity_matrix()` in `new_views.py` | Player-vs-player similarity: heat map matrix showing % winner agreement between each pair, ranked table of most similar pairs (winner %, score %) |
142: | **Ranking Evolution** | `ranking_evolution.html` | `_build_ranking_evolution()` in `html.py` | Interactive Canvas line chart: toggleable per-player display of rank position over time (inverted Y-axis), tooltip on hover, current standings table with leader distance |
143: | **Boldometro** | `boldometer.html` | `_build_boldometer()` in `html.py` | Interactive Canvas scatter plot: x-axis = boldness (avg goals vs bolao avg), y-axis = avg points per game. 4 quadrants (Efficient/Risky/Conservative/Bold). Tooltip on hover with player profile. Explanation section. Top-3 boldest players table. |
144: | **Momentum** | `momentum.html` | `_build_momentum()` in `html.py` | Current streaks per player (hot/cold) with visual bars, longest streak records table, podium for hottest and coldest current streaks, explanation of streak rules |
145: | **Day Winners** | `day_winners.html` | `_build_day_winners()` in `html.py` | Per-day match analysis: the match with most correct predictions, most wrong predictions, and most divided predictions for each game day |
146: 
147: #### Navigation
148: 
149: All pages share a **bottom navigation bar** with 5 links:
150: - `index.html` (Inicio)
151: - `arena.html` (Arena)
152: - `zebras.html` (Zebras)
153: - `palpites.html` (Palpites)
154: - `bolao_xray.html` (Raio-X)
155: 
156: The dashboard `index.html` also has a **3x3 quick-link grid**:
157: - `tabela_real.html` (Grupos)
158: - `rodadas.html` (Rodadas)
159: - `times.html` (Times)
160: - `similaridade.html` (Sosia)
161: - `palpites.html` (Palpites)
162: - `ranking_evolution.html` (Evolucao)
163: - `boldometer.html` (Boldometro)
164: - `zebras.html` (Zebras)
165: - `bolao_xray.html` (Raio-X)
166: 
167: ---
168: 
169: ### 4. Chart / Visualization Libraries Used
170: 
171: **No external charting libraries (no Chart.js, D3.js, Plotly, etc.).** All visualizations are implemented using:
172: 
173: 1. **HTML/CSS bar charts** -- Pure CSS `<div>` elements with percentage widths for bar charts (distribution, comparisons, difficulty rankings)
174: 2. **HTML/CSS heatmaps** -- Grid of colored `<div>` elements for the player x day heatmap and the per-match prediction heatmap
175: 3. **HTML/CSS tables** -- For all ranking tables, matrices, and data grids (with zebra striping, rank highlights via CSS classes)
176: 4. **HTML `<canvas>`** -- Using **raw JavaScript Canvas API** for two interactive charts:
177:    - `ranking_evolution.html` -- Line chart of rank position over time with toggleable players, tooltips, grid lines
178:    - `boldometer.html` -- Scatter plot with quadrant backgrounds, dot rendering with initials, tooltip on hover
179: 
180: The JavaScript code is inline within each HTML page (no external JS files). The styling uses CSS custom properties driven by the championship's `ThemeConfig` (dark theme by default, mobile-first responsive design).
181: 
182: There is also **client-side JavaScript** for:
183: - Dropdown filter menus with checkboxes in `palpites.html`
184: - Player comparison selector in `arena.html`
185: - Toggleable player lines in `ranking_evolution.html`
186: - Interactive hover tooltips in charts
187: 
188: **Backend Python visualization:** `matplotlib>=3.10.3` is listed as a dependency in `pyproject.toml` but is **not currently used** in any of the report generation code. It may be used for future or optional features. `streamlit>=1.45.1` is also listed but **not used** in the current codebase.
189: </task_result>
190: </task>
191: ```

237: Possible new views that don't repeat existing ones:
238: 
239: 1. **"Cenarios" (Scenarios)** - What-if analysis: "If remaining matches go a certain way, who would win?" - This is a classic in bolao/sweepstakes. Show possible final rankings based on different scenarios.
240: 
241: 2. **"Confronto Direto" (Head-to-Head)** - A dedicated head-to-head view that shows a complete player-vs-player win/loss/draw record, not just the arena comparison. Like a league table of head-to-head results between each pair of players.
242: 
243: 3. **"Palpite do Dia" (Pick of the Day)** - Which predictions stood out each day? Best call of the day, worst miss, most creative score guess. Curated by algorithm.
244: 
245: 4. **"Vies" (Bias Analysis)** - Each player's bias tendencies: do they tend to predict more goals than average? Do they favor certain teams? Home team bias? Over/under bias?
246: 
247: 5. **"Mercado de Palpites" (Prediction Market)** - Show which games have the most consensus vs. most disagreement among players. Like a prediction market view - games where everyone agrees vs. games where opinions are divided.
248: 
249: 6. **"Historico do Boleiro" (Player History across championships)** - Since the config supports multiple championships (2025 Club World Cup, 2026 World Cup), a cross-championship player comparison would be unique.
250: 
251: 7. **"Calendario" (Calendar View)** - A calendar/schedule view showing all matches on their dates with predictions and results in a compact calendar layout.
252: 
253: 8. **"Eficiencia por Rodada" (Efficiency per Round)** - Not just points per round, but efficiency: points scored / max possible points per round, visualized differently from the round matrix.
254: 
255: 9. **"Arvore de Decisoes" (Decision Tree for Playoffs)** - For knockout stages, show the playoff bracket with each player's predicted path and how it diverges from reality.
256: 
257: 10. **"Oponente Mais Dificil" (Toughest Opponent)** - For each player, which other player is their "nemesis" (the one they consistently lose to in head-to-head)?

270: 
271: 2. **"Quem Confiar?" (Who to Trust?)** - For each team, show which player has been most accurate predicting that team's matches. Like "if you want to know how Brazil will do, ask Player X."
272: 
273: 3. **"Janela de Palpites" (Prediction Window/Timing)** - Using the prediction_timing data, show when each player submitted their picks relative to match kickoff. Did early predictors do better than late ones? A timeline visualization.
274: 
275: 4. **"Duelo de Gigantes" (Clash of Titans)** - For the top matches (top of the table clashes, rivalries), show a special comparison of how players predicted those key matches.
276: 
277: 5. **"Moda vs Mediana" (Mode vs Median)** - Show the most common prediction (mode) for each match vs the actual result and vs the median. Like "the crowd's wisdom" analysis.
278: 
279: 6. **"Matriz de Correlacao" (Correlation Matrix)** - Who predicts most like whom? A full correlation matrix (different from the similarity matrix which shows agreement % - this would be about score correlation).

292: 
293: 4. **"Correlacao de Palpites" (Pick Correlation)** - A scatter/correlation matrix of players' score predictions. Different from Similaridade (winner agreement %) because this would be about exact score correlation.

352: 💭 **PENSAMENTO DO AGENTE:**
353: The user chose "Arquétipos (Player Personas)" - classifying each player into archetypes based on their prediction patterns.
354: 
355: Let me think about this more deeply. I need to plan what data to use and how to build this.
356: 
357: Available data per player:
358: - Boldness index (boldness_index.csv) - how much their score predictions deviate from the average
359: - Consistency (consistency.csv) - streaks, consistency of performance
360: - Zebras hit (upset_tracker.csv) - did they predict upsets?
361: - Team accuracy (team_accuracy.csv) - which teams they predict well
362: - Goal error by team (goal_error_by_team.csv) - MAE per team
363: - Round-by-round points (round_by_round.csv) - performance over time
364: - Scoring distribution per player
365: - Prediction timing (prediction_timing.csv) - when they submit
366: - Overall stats: points, avg, aproveitamento
367: 
368: Possible archetypes:
369: 
370: 1. **O Favoriteiro (The Favorite Follower)** - Always picks favorites, rarely hits zebras, low boldness, high accuracy on favorites
371: 2. **O Zebreiro (The Zebra Hunter)** - Loves predicting upsets, high boldness, low overall accuracy but high zebra count
372: 3. **O Otimista (The Optimist)** - Always predicts lots of goals (high scoring predictions), high goal error overestimation
373: 4. **O Pessimista (The Pessimist)** - Predicts low-scoring games, underestimates goals
374: 5. **O Conservador (The Conservative)** - Predicts close to the average, low boldness, medium accuracy
375: 6. **O Cientista (The Scientist)** - Most accurate overall, data-driven predictions, consistent
376: 7. **O Tardio (The Latecomer)** - Submits predictions last minute, varies in accuracy
377: 8. **O Madrugador (The Early Bird)** - Submits predictions well in advance
378: 9. **O Especialista (The Specialist)** - Very good with specific teams, bad with others
379: 10. **O Consistente (The Consistent)** - Always scores around the same, no wild swings
380:

401: ### Championship directories under the gold layer
402: 
403: There are **two** championship directories, but only one has gold data:

407: | `2026_world_cup` | `src/championships/2026_world_cup/data/gold/first_round/` | **Yes** -- full gold-layer CSVs |

581: | `away_team` | Africa do Sul |

583: | `away_goals_bol` | 0.0 |
584: | `home_goals_real` | 2.0 |
585: | `away_goals_real` | 0.0 |
586: | `home_pen_bol` | (empty) |
587: | `away_pen_bol` | (empty) |
588: | `home_pen_real` | (empty) |
589: | `away_pen_real` | (empty) |
590: | `resultado_bol_placar` | 3 x 0 |
591: | `resultado_bol_time` | Mexico |
592: | `resultado_real_placar` | 2 x 0 |
593: | `resultado_real_time` | Mexico |
594: | `time_elapsed` | finished |
595: | `who` | Andre Bonito |
596: | `pontos` | 10 |
597: | `criterio` | 2-Vencedor + gols de um time (erro <= 2) |
598: | `valido` | 1 |
599: | `1-Placar exato` | False |
600: | `2-Vencedor + gols de um time (erro <= 2)` | True |
601: | `3-Vencedor + diferenca de gols (erro <= 2)` | False |
602: | `4-Empate c/ erro total <= 2` | False |
603: | `5-Vencedor + gols de um time (erro > 2)` | False |
604: | `6-Vencedor correto` | False |
605: | `7-Qualquer outro empate` | False |
606: | `8-Gols de um time` | False |
607: | `9-Nenhum acerto` | False |
608: | `99-Sem jogo` | False |
609: 
610: **Description:** The full per-player, per-match prediction dataset. `1afase_all` = all matches; `1afase_valido_all` = only valid (completed) matches. The `1-...` through `99-...` columns are one-hot scoring criteria flags.
611: 
612: ---
613: 
614: #### 11. `playoffs_strikers.csv` (34 rows)
615: 
616: | Column | Sample value (Andre Bonito) |
617: |---|---|
618: | `boleiro` | Andre Bonito |
619: | `striker` | Cristiano Ronaldo |
620: 
621: **Description:** Who each player picked as their playoff (knockout) top striker.
622: 
623: ---
624: 
625: #### 12. `playoffs_scored.csv` (1 row -- header only, no data)
626: 
627: | Column | Description |
628: |---|---|
629: | `boleiro` | Player name |
630: | `phase` | Playoff phase |
631: | `team_picked` | Team they picked |
632: | `team_actual` | Actual team |
633: | `correct` | Boolean |
634: | `points` | Points earned |
635: 
636: **Description:** Placeholder -- no playoff data yet.
637: 
638: ---
639: 
640: #### 13. `strikers_scored.csv` (1 row -- header only, no data)
641: 
642: | Column | Description |
643: |---|---|
644: | `boleiro` | Player name |
645: | `striker_picked` | Striker they picked |
646: | `actual_top_scorer` | Actual top scorer |
647: | `correct` | Boolean |
648: | `points` | Points earned |
649: 
650: **Description:** Placeholder -- no top-scorer data yet.
651: 
652: ---
653: 
654: #### 14. Per-player files (examples)
655: 
656: **`group_phase_<Player>.csv`** -- Same schema as `1afase_all.csv` above, but filtered to a single player. Contains all their match-by-match predictions for the group phase.
657: 
658: **`striker_<Player>.csv`** -- Simple file, just 2 columns:
659: 
660: | Column | Sample (Joao Henrique) |
661: |---|---|
662: | `boleiro` | Joao Henrique |
663: | `striker` | Mbappe |
664: 
665: ---
666: 
667: ### Key columns for classifying player archetypes
668: 
669: For **player archetype classification**, the most useful gold-layer files and their columns are:
670: 
671: | File | Key archetype signals |
672: |---|---|
673: | **`boldness_index.csv`** | `boldness_score`, `avg_total_goals_bol`, `extreme_score_pct` -- risk-seeking vs conservative predictor |
674: | **`consistency.csv`** | `streak_type`, `streak_length`, `running_avg_5` -- streaky vs steady performer |
675: | **`upset_tracker.csv`** | `is_upset`, `num_correct` -- contrarian vs consensus-follower |
676: | **`team_accuracy.csv`** | `accuracy_pct`, `correct_winner`, `exact_score` -- precision profile per team/role |
677: | **`goal_error_by_team.csv`** | `mae`, `goal_bias` -- over-predictor vs under-predictor per team |
678: | **`round_by_round.csv`** | `points`, `cumulative_points`, `rank` -- early starter vs late bloomer |
679: | **`prediction_timing.csv`** | `lead_days` -- early submitter vs last-minute predictor |
680: | **`playoffs_strikers.csv`** | `striker` -- favorite player choice (star-chaser vs unique pick) |
681: | **`1afase_all.csv` / `1afase_valido_all.csv`** | `criterio`, `pontos`, and the `1-...` through `9-...` one-hot flags -- exact-score specialist vs safe-bet player |
682: </task_result>
683: </task>
684: ```
685: 
686: ---
687: 
688: ### 🤖 AGENTE (Turno 4):
689: 
690: 💭 **PENSAMENTO DO AGENTE:**
691: Now I have a very comprehensive understanding of the data available. Let me design the "Arquétipos (Player Personas)" view.
692: 
693: The idea is to classify each player into a persona based on their prediction patterns. I'll use multiple data signals.
694: 
695: Here are the archetypes I can define based on available data:
696: 
697: ### Proposed Archetypes
698: 
699: 1. **O Zebreiro (The Zebra Hunter)** 🦓
700:    - Signs: High number of zebras predicted correctly, high boldness score, low crowd-agreement
701:    - Data: upset_tracker (players_correct count), boldness_index (boldness_score)
702: 
703: 2. **O Cientista (The Scientist)** 🧪
704:    - Signs: Highest overall accuracy, consistent performance, balanced predictions
705:    - Data: 1afase_valido_all (avg pontos), consistency (running_avg), team_accuracy
706: 
707: 3. **O Conservador (The Conservative)** 🛡️
708:    - Signs: Low boldness score, near-average predictions, few extremes
709:    - Data: boldness_index (low boldness_score, low extreme_score_pct)
710: 
711: 4. **O Otimista (The Optimist)** 🌟
712:    - Signs: Predicts more goals than average, positive goal_bias
713:    - Data: boldness_index (high avg_total_goals_bol), goal_error_by_team (positive goal_bias on average)
714: 
715: 5. **O Pessimista (The Pessimist)** ☁️
716:    - Signs: Predicts fewer goals than average, negative goal_bias
717:    - Data: boldness_index (low avg_total_goals_bol), goal_error_by_team (negative goal_bias on average)
718: 
719: 6. **O Especialista (The Specialist)** 🎯
720:    - Signs: Very high accuracy on specific teams, low on others, high variance in team accuracy
721:    - Data: team_accuracy (high variance in accuracy_pct across teams)
722: 
723: 7. **O Consistente (The Consistent One)** 📊
724:    - Signs: Low variance in points per round, steady rank, long hit streaks
725:    - Data: round_by_round (low variance in points), consistency (longest hit streak)
726: 
727: 8. **O Montanha-Russa (The Rollercoaster)** 🎢
728:    - Signs: High variance in points per round, alternates hot/cold streaks
729:    - Data: round_by_round (high variance in points), consistency (alternating streak types)
730: 
731: 9. **O Madrugador (The Early Bird)** 🐦
732:    - Signs: Submits predictions many days before first match
733:    - Data: prediction_timing (high lead_days)
734: 
735: 10. **O Tardio (The Latecomer)** ⏰
736:     - Signs: Submits predictions last minute / after first match started
737:     - Data: prediction_timing (low or zero lead_days)
738: 
739: 11. **O Placarzeiro (The Exact-Score Specialist)** 🎯
740:     - Signs: Higher than average proportion of exact-score hits
741:     - Data: 1afase_valido_all (count of "1-Placar exato" = True), or proportion of exact scores
742: 
743: 12. **O Craque (The Star)** 👑
744:     - Signs: Overall top performer - high rank, high points, high accuracy across the board
745:     - Data: multiple signals
746: 
747: Actually, some of these are too many. Let me refine to a core set of ~7-8 that are most distinct and data-supported:
748: 
749: 1. **O Cientista** - Highest accuracy, balanced, consistent top performer
750: 2. **O Zebreiro** - Zebra hunter, contrarian, high boldness
751: 3. **O Conservador** - Safe predictions, low boldness, follows the crowd
752: 4. **O Otimista** - Predicts goal-fests, overestimates goals
753: 5. **O Pessimista** - Predicts low scores, underestimates
754: 6. **O Especialista** - Niche expert in specific teams
755: 7. **O Montanha-Russa** - Wild swings in performance
756: 8. **O Placarzeiro** - Exact-score specialist
757: 
758: And each player gets a PRIMARY archetype (the one that fits best) plus secondary traits shown as badges.
759: 
760: Let me now plan the implementation:
761: 
762: ### Plan
763: 
764: 1. New view: **Arquétipos.html** (arquetipos.html)
765: 2. Python generator in `src/core/reports/new_views.py` (or a new file)
766: 3. Uses gold data to classify each player
767: 4. Display:
768:    - Header: "Arquétipos do Bolão" with explanation
769:    - Grid of player cards, each showing:
770:      - Player name and avatar/initial
771:      - Primary archetype with emoji and name
772:      - Archetype description (1-2 lines)
773:      - Archetype score (how strongly they fit, 0-100%)
774:      - Key stats that support the classification
775:      - Secondary traits as small badges
776:    - Maybe a radar/spider chart per player showing their trait scores
777: 5. Bottom nav + link from dashboard
778: 
779: Let me now think about the math for classification:
780: 
781: #### Classification Logic
782: 
783: **Boldness Score** (from boldness_index.csv):
784: - Normalize boldness_score across players (0-1)
785: - High = Zebreiro/Otimista tendency
786: - Low = Conservador/Pessimista tendency
787: 
788: **Goal Bias** (from goal_error_by_team.csv):
789: - Average goal_bias across all teams per player
790: - Positive = Otimista
791: - Negative = Pessimista
792: 
793: **Consistency** (from consistency.csv):
794: - Variance of running_avg_5 - high variance = Montanha-Russa
795: - Longest hit streak = Consistente
796: - Ratio of hit to miss streaks
797: 
798: **Team Accuracy Variance** (from team_accuracy.csv):
799: - Variance of accuracy_pct across teams
800: - High = Especialista (good with some, bad with others)
801: - Low = Generalista
802: 
803: **Exact Score Rate** (from 1afase_valido_all.csv):
804: - Count of "1-Placar exato" / total matches
805: - High = Placarzeiro
806: 
807: **Zebra Rate** (from upset_tracker.csv):
808: - Count of upsets they predicted correctly
809: - High = Zebreiro
810: 
811: Let me plan the file structure:
812: 
813: - `src/core/reports/arquetipos.py` - New module with classification logic + HTML generation
814: - Or add to `src/core/reports/new_views.py`
815: 
816: Actually, looking at the existing code structure, the HTML generation is split:
817: - `src/core/reports/html.py` - Main HTML generation
818: - `src/core/reports/new_views.py` - Newer views (times, palpites grid, rodadas, similarity, group standings)
819: - `src/core/reports/dashboard.py` - Dashboard
820: 
821: I think adding to `new_views.py` makes sense since it's the newer views file, OR a new file `arquetipos.py`.
822: 
823: Now let me present the plan to the user.
824: 
825: Let me think about something more concrete. The user wants something "novo mas que nao se repita". The archetype view is novel because:
826: 1. No existing view classifies players into behavioral personas
827: 2. It synthesizes data from many different CSVs into a single classification
828: 3. It tells a story about each player's style, not just their score
829: 4. It's fun and engaging - people love being categorized
830: 
831: I should ask about a few things before finalizing:
832: - Whether to add to existing new_views.py or a new file
833: - How many archetypes
834: - Visual style preferences
835: 
836: Actually, let me read the new_views.py to understand the patterns first.
837: 
838: 📄 **RESPOSTA DO AGENTE:**
839: Ótima escolha! **Arquétipos (Player Personas)** é algo totalmente novo — nenhuma view existente classifica o *estilo* de cada jogador. Vou ler os arquivos de geração de HTML para planejar a implementação.

870: 26: 
871: 27: def _page_frame(config: ChampionshipConfig, title: str, body: str, back_link: str = "", active_nav: str = "") -> str:
872: 28:     from src.core.reports.html import _CSS_BASE, _bottom_nav_html
873: 29:     tz = pytz.timezone(config.timezone)
874: 30:     now_str = datetime.now(tz).strftime("%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S")
875: 31:     back_html = ""

877: 33:     if back_link:

879: 35:         idx = back_link.rfind("index.html")
880: 36:         if idx >= 0:
881: 37:             nav_prefix = back_link[:idx]
882: 38:     return f"""<!DOCTYPE html>
883: 39: <html lang="pt-BR">
884: 40: <head>

886: 42:     <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">

889: 45:     {config.theme.to_css_vars()}
890: 46:     {_CSS_BASE}
891: 47:     </style>
892: 48: </head>
893: 49: <body>
894: 50: {back_html}
895: 51: {body}
896: 52: <div style="text-align:center;padding:2rem 1rem 5rem;color:var(--text-muted);font-size:0.75rem;">
897: 53:     atualizado \u00e0s {now_str}
898: 54: </div>
899: 55: {_bottom_nav_html(active_nav, nav_prefix)}
900: 56: </body>
901: 57: </html>"""

906: 62: # ------------------------------------------------------------------
907: 63: 
908: 64: def _build_group_standings(config: ChampionshipConfig) -> str:
909: 65:     """Real tournament group standings from games.csv results."""
910: 66:     if not config.groups:
911: 67:         return "<div class='hero'><h1>\U0001f3c6 Tabela Real</h1><div class='subtitle'>Nenhum grupo configurado</div></div>"
912: 68: 
913: 69:     df_games = pd.read_csv(config.games_file, sep=",")
914: 70:     if df_games.empty:
915: 71:         return _page_frame(config, "Tabela Real", "<div class='hero'><h1>\U0001f3c6 Tabela Real</h1><div class='subtitle'>Nenhum jogo encontrado</div></div>")
916: 72: 
917: 73:     # Filter group stage matches (rounds 1, 2, 3)
918: 74:     group_rounds = ["1", "2", "3"]
919: 75:     df_group = df_games[df_games["round"].astype(str).str.strip().isin(group_rounds)].copy()
920: 76: 
921: 77:     html = '<div class="hero"><h1>\U0001f3c6 Tabela Real dos Grupos</h1><div class="subtitle">Classifica\u00e7\u00e3o do campeonato real</div></div>'
922: 78: 
923: 79:     for grp in config.groups:
924: 80:         group_name = grp.get("name", "?")
925: 81:         teams = grp.get("teams", [])
926: 82:         standings = _compute_group_standings(teams, df_group)
927: 83: 
928: 84:         if not standings:
929: 85:             html += f'<div class="section"><div class="section-title">\U0001f3c6 Grupo {group_name}</div><div class="card"><div class="empty-state">Nenhum jogo encontrado para este grupo</div></div></div>'
930: 86:             continue
931: 87: 
932: 88:         # Build table
933: 89:         # Sort: points desc, GD desc, GF desc
934: 90:         standings.sort(key=lambda r: (-r["pts"], -(r["gf"] - r["ga"]), -r["gf"]))
935: 91: 
936: 92:         table_rows = ""
937: 93:         for i, row in enumerate(standings):
938: 94:             rank_class = f' class="rank-{i+1}"' if i < 3 else ""
939: 95:             gd = row["gf"] - row["ga"]
940: 96:             gd_str = f"+{gd}" if gd > 0 else str(gd)
941: 97:             table_rows += f"""<tr{rank_class}>
942: 98:                 <td>{i+1}</td>
943: 99:                 <td><strong>{row["team"]}</strong></td>
944: 100:                 <td>{row["p"]}</td>
945:

1072: 25: 
1073: 26: def _load_gold_data(config: ChampionshipConfig) -> pd.DataFrame:
1074: 27:     """Load gold data — prefers valid, falls back to all predictions.
1075: 28:
